mapping.a_element: change of variables mapping the hard equation onto a solvable target (curved radial equation -> flat spherical Bessel equation)
mapping.b_element: change of variables mapping the hard equation onto a solvable target (nonlinear goldfish ODEs -> free linear motion)

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic strategy 'solve a hard equation by transforming it into a simpler solvable one', which is not shared transferable machinery. Paper A uses an Olver/WKB uniform-asymptotic construction that approximately matches the action of a curved second-order linear ODE onto the flat Bessel equation through a turning point, whereas paper B uses an exact algebraic change of variables (polynomial coefficients to its zeros) to linearize a nonlinear N-body ODE system to free motion; the driving mechanisms (approximate turning-point asymptotics vs. exact roots-to-coefficients integrable-systems linearization) are entirely different.
